---

title: "Platform Narrative"

---

Platform Overview

The ML model deployment platform provides a specification-first, notebook-driven foundation for training, promotion, and infrastructure-as-code governance. The system prioritizes self-contained documentation, traceability through MLflow, and local-to-cloud parity for infrastructure and operations.

Platform architecture

The platform is organized into five core domains:

1. System Components & Roles — Core entities (Repository, DocumentationSeries, MLflow, OpenTofu) and their relationships

2. Execution & Data Flow — Workflows (notebook execution, model promotion, artifact tracking) and state machines

3. Deployment & Infrastructure — Local emulation and cloud topology with parity constraints

4. Governance & Safety — Promotion gates and spec-first safety controls

5. Learning Paths — Role-specific reading guides for engineers and researchers

See detailed breakdowns below.

---

1. System Components & Roles

Core Entities

The platform's architecture is built on five core entities and their relationships:

  • Repository: The root scope; manages phases (specification_first vs. implementation_enabled), tracks deployment profiles, and orchestrates CI/CD.

- DocumentationSeries: Self-contained, hierarchical docs that serve as the source of truth for system behavior.

- OpenTofuConfiguration: Infrastructure-as-code generated by Nix/Terranix; supports both local emulation and cloud deployment profiles.

- MLflowTrackingServer: Centralized model and experiment tracking with PostgreSQL backend and S3/Floci artifact store.

- ModelArtifact: Versioned model packages with promotion gates (DEV → UAT → REGRESSION → PROD).

- PromotionStage: Discrete approval and quality gate levels for production safety.

Entity Relationships and Responsibilities

This introduction-level narrative intentionally keeps entity relationships concise.

For the full relationship map and responsibility-level structure, see

12_system_interaction_analysis.qmd.

Component Interaction Overview

The system orchestrates seven interaction domains:

1. Notebook Intake: Immutable qmd/ipynb sources with export guards

2. Execution Dispatch: Route to local, Slurm, or Kubernetes executors

3. MLflow Traceability: Track params, metrics, artifacts, model versions

4. Data Lineage: Link datasets, transformations, features, model artifacts, and promotion events end-to-end

5. Artifact Management: Package, version, and store trained models

6. Security & Capability Validation: Enforce role/capability checks for data access, model operations, local/cloud execution, infrastructure changes, and promotion actions

7. Promotion & Governance: Apply gates, approvals, and quality checks before production

---

2. Execution & Data Flow

Key Workflows

The platform implements four primary workflows, each with distinct decision points and state transitions:

A. Notebook Execution Workflow (EX-01)

sequenceDiagram


    actor Engineer


    participant Notebook as Quarto QMD


    participant Intake as Notebook Intake


    participant Dispatch as Execution Dispatch


    participant Executor as Local/Slurm/K8s


    participant MLflow as MLflow Server


    


    Engineer->>Notebook: Run cell


    Notebook->>Intake: Extract execution request


    Intake->>Intake: Validate contract


    Intake->>Dispatch: Dispatch (immutable contract)


    Dispatch->>Executor: Route to target


    Executor->>MLflow: Log params, metrics


    Executor->>Executor: Train model


    MLflow->>MLflow: Record artifact URI


    Executor->>MLflow: Log model version


    MLflow-->>Engineer: Run completed

B. Model Promotion Workflow (EX-02 → EX-03)

sequenceDiagram


    actor Approver


    participant Artifact as ModelArtifact


    participant Gate as Promotion Gate


    participant Registry as MLflow Registry


    participant Infra as Infrastructure


    


    Approver->>Gate: Request promotion


    Gate->>Gate: Check quality gates


    Gate->>Gate: Verify approval status


    alt Approval granted


        Gate->>Registry: Update stage


        Registry->>Infra: Prepare environment


        Infra->>Infra: Apply infrastructure changes


        Infra-->>Approver: Promotion complete


    else Approval denied


        Gate-->>Approver: Promotion blocked


    end

C. Artifact Tracking Workflow

sequenceDiagram


    participant Training as Training Run


    participant MLflow as MLflow Server


    participant Store as PostgreSQL + S3/Floci


    participant Records as Artifact Records


    


    Training->>MLflow: Start run


    Training->>MLflow: Log params


    Training->>MLflow: Log metrics


    Training->>Store: Save model binary


    MLflow->>Store: Record artifact path


    MLflow->>Records: Create model version


    Records->>Records: Initialize stage as DEV


    Records-->>Training: Artifact ready

State Machines

Repository Governance Phase Machine

stateDiagram-v2


    [*] --> specification_first


    


    specification_first --> implementation_enabled: explicit_confirmation + quality_gate_passed


    


    implementation_enabled --> implementation_enabled: re-check confirmation


    


    note right of specification_first


        Phase 1: Spec must be reviewed


        Quality gate must pass


        Explicit user confirmation required


    end note


    


    note right of implementation_enabled


        Phase 2: Implementation active


        Future checks still require confirmation


        Code and spec must stay aligned


    end note

Model Promotion State Machine

stateDiagram-v2


    [*] --> DEV


    


    DEV --> UAT: passes_smoke_tests


    UAT --> REGRESSION: passes_uat_suite + no_data_leakage + meets_minimum_metrics


    REGRESSION --> PROD: passes_regression_suite + no_performance_regression + security_scan_passed


    


    note right of DEV


        Initial artifact stage


        Available for smoke testing


    end note


    


    note right of UAT


        User acceptance testing


        Production-representative sample data


    end note


    


    note right of REGRESSION


        Full regression suite


        Data leakage and performance checks


    end note


    


    note right of PROD


        Production live serving


        Security scan passed


        PyTorch optimizations applied


    end note

---

3. Deployment & Infrastructure

Local Emulation Stack

The local development environment mirrors production infrastructure using Docker Compose:

graph LR


    subgraph Local["Local Development (Docker Compose)"]


        Traefik["Traefik
Reverse Proxy
:80 :443"]


        MLflow["MLflow Server
:5000"]


        Postgres["PostgreSQL
Backend Store
:5432"]


        Floci["Floci
S3 Emulator
:4566"]


        Dev["Developer Container
Python + Notebooks
Mount: ./"]


        K3s["K3s
Local Kubernetes
:6443"]


        Slurm["SLURM Emulator
Job Scheduler
:6817"]


    end


    


    Dev -->|tracking URI| Traefik


    Traefik -->|route| MLflow


    MLflow -->|backend store| Postgres


    MLflow -->|artifact store| Floci


    Dev -->|execute local| Dev


    Dev -->|dispatch| K3s


    Dev -->|dispatch| Slurm


    


    style Local fill:#e1f5ff

Cloud Deployment Topology

Production deployment uses AWS managed services with Kubernetes orchestration:

graph LR


    subgraph Cloud["AWS Cloud (Kubernetes + Managed Services)"]


        Traefik["Traefik
Ingress Controller"]


        EKS["EKS Cluster
Kubernetes"]


        MLflow_Pod["MLflow Pod
Serving"]


        RDS["RDS Aurora PostgreSQL
Backend Store"]


        S3["S3 Bucket
Artifact Store"]


        Lambda["Lambda.ai
SLURM Training
External"]


    end


    


    Internet["Internet
HTTPS"]


    


    Internet -->|HTTPS| Traefik


    Traefik -->|route| EKS


    EKS -->|runs| MLflow_Pod


    MLflow_Pod -->|backend| RDS


    MLflow_Pod -->|artifacts| S3


    EKS -->|dispatch training| Lambda


    Lambda -->|results to| S3


    


    style Cloud fill:#f3e5f5

Infrastructure Parity Constraints

Local and cloud deployments must maintain parity across:

| Component | Local | Cloud | Parity Check |

|-----------|-------|-------|-------------|

| Reverse Proxy | Traefik (Docker) | Traefik Ingress (K8s) | Same routing rules |

| Backend Store | PostgreSQL (Docker) | RDS Aurora PostgreSQL | Same schema, connection pool |

| Artifact Store | Floci S3-compatible | AWS S3 | Same S3 API surface |

| Compute | Local + Docker + K3s + SLURM emulator | EKS + Lambda.ai SLURM | Contract-driven dispatch |

---

4. Governance & Safety

Promotion Gate Architecture

Model promotion requires passing progressive quality gates:

graph TD


    A["Artifact Created
Stage: DEV"] 


    


    B["Smoke Tests"]


    C{Pass?}


    D["Stage: UAT"]


    


    E["UAT Suite"]


    F["Data Leakage Check"]


    G["Min Metrics Met"]


    H{All Pass?}


    I["Stage: REGRESSION"]


    


    J["Regression Suite"]


    K["Perf Regression"]


    L["Security Scan"]


    M["PyTorch Optimizations"]


    N{All Pass?}


    O["Approval Gate"]


    P{Approved?}


    Q["Stage: PROD"]


    


    A --> B --> C


    C -->|Yes| D --> E & F & G


    E & F & G --> H


    H -->|Yes| I --> J & K & L & M


    J & K & L & M --> N


    N -->|Yes| O --> P


    P -->|Yes| Q


    


    C -->|No| A


    H -->|No| D


    N -->|No| I


    P -->|No| O


    


    style A fill:#fff3e0


    style D fill:#e3f2fd


    style I fill:#f3e5f5


    style Q fill:#c8e6c9

Spec-First Governance

The repository lifecycle enforces specification-first principles:

stateDiagram-v2


    [*] --> SpecificationFirst


    


    SpecificationFirst --> AwaitingQualityGate: spec_written


    AwaitingQualityGate --> AwaitingConfirmation: quality_gate_passed


    AwaitingConfirmation --> ImplementationEnabled: explicit_confirmation


    


    ImplementationEnabled --> Synced: code_matches_spec


    ImplementationEnabled --> OutOfSync: drift_detected


    OutOfSync --> ImplementationEnabled: weed_audit_resolved


    


    note right of SpecificationFirst


        All behavior must be specified first


        Before code is written


    end note


    


    note right of AwaitingQualityGate


        Spec undergoes quality review


        Consistency and completeness checks


    end note


    


    note right of AwaitingConfirmation


        User must explicitly confirm


        Readiness to move to implementation


    end note


    


    note right of ImplementationEnabled


        Code implementation phase active


        Continuous sync checks required


    end note


    


    note right of OutOfSync


        Weed audits detect spec-code drift


        Resolving divergences keeps system healthy


    end note

---

5. Learning Paths

Software Engineer Learning Path

Engineers focused on infrastructure, deployment, and operations should follow this progression:

graph TD


    A["Start Here:
Web UI Contracts
nbs/05_webui_contracts.qmd"]


    B["Execution Adapters
nbs/08_execution_backends.qmd"]


    C["MLflow Parity Setup
nbs/07_mlflow_parity.qmd"]


    D["Infrastructure MCP
nbs/14_infrastructure_mcp.qmd"]


    E["OpenTofu / Terranix
nbs/13_opentofu_infra.qmd"]


    F["AWS Emulator
nbs/15_aws_emulator.qmd"]


    G["Advanced:
System Interaction
nbs/12_system_interaction_analysis.qmd"]


    


    A -->|understand interfaces| B


    B -->|set up local parity| C


    C -->|explore observability| D


    D -->|learn infrastructure code| E


    E -->|debug emulation| F


    F -->|master system patterns| G


    


    style A fill:#fff3e0


    style G fill:#c8e6c9

ML Researcher Learning Path

Researchers focused on model development, training, and promotion should follow this progression:

graph TD


    A["Start Here:
Platform Architecture
This page"]


    B["Vertical Slice Example
nbs/06_vertical_slice.qmd"]


    C["Notebook Intake
nbs/09_notebook_intake.qmd"]


    D["Data & Training
nbs/02_data.qmd + nbs/03_model_training.qmd"]


    E["MLflow Tracking
nbs/07_mlflow_parity.qmd"]


    F["Model Promotion
nbs/17_governance_gates.qmd"]


    G["Advanced:
System Interaction
nbs/12_system_interaction_analysis.qmd"]


    


    A -->|concrete walkthrough| B


    B -->|capture experiments| C


    C -->|train models| D


    D -->|track & compare| E


    E -->|promote safely| F


    F -->|understand governance| G


    


    style A fill:#fff3e0


    style G fill:#c8e6c9

---

Implementation steps

1. Validate contracts — Immutable notebook intake and execution request structure

2. Route execution — Dispatch through local, Slurm, or Kubernetes adapters

3. Record traceability — Link all outcomes to MLflow runs and artifact records

4. Apply gates — Enforce promotion stage transitions with quality checks

5. Verify parity — Ensure local emulation and cloud deployment remain synchronized

Trade-offs

  • Notebook-first workflows improve discoverability but require strict immutability and export guards

- Local emulation reduces cloud-risk iteration cost but cannot fully replicate all production failure modes

- Spec-first governance improves consistency but can lag implementation if drift checks are skipped

- Extensive diagramming aids comprehension but requires ongoing maintenance as the system evolves

Security considerations

  • Keep MCP integrations read-first and least-privilege by default

- Enforce immutable notebook references for all execution

- Treat cloud deployment credentials and runtime secrets as externally managed inputs

- Model version source validation must be enabled in production (S3 URI regex constraints)

- All MLflow tracking must use secure backend store connections

Examples of use

  • Local reference slice execution: Run the vertical slice example with MLflow-linked run visibility

- Backend payload mapping: Submit training jobs to Slurm/Kubernetes with normalized request contracts

- Notebook intake validation: Parse and validate immutable execution contracts before dispatch

- Promotion workflow: Move models through DEV → UAT → REGRESSION → PROD with gate enforcement

Engineers focused on infrastructure, deployment, and operations should follow this progression:

Web UI Contracts

System Interaction

---

Cross-Cutting Concepts

High-Level Introduction

This platform serves hedge-fund ML engineers who require:

1. Discoverability: Self-contained documentation that doesn't require repository browsing

2. Reproducibility: Immutable notebook execution contracts backed by spec-first governance

3. Traceability: Complete MLflow linkage from training through promotion

4. Parity: Local emulation that mirrors production infrastructure and behavior

5. Safety: Progressive promotion gates with quality checks and approvals
